title: WOS AI Integration Document
description: A WOS AI Integration Document per the WOS AI Integration Specification v1.0 (Layer 2). Targets a WOS Kernel Document and declares agent registration, deontic constraints (permission/prohibition/obligation/right), autonomy levels with impact-level caps, Formspec-as-validator pattern, confidence framework with decay, fallback chains, decision drift detection, AI-specific oversight extensions, volume constraints, agent-specific review sampling, agent disclosure, and Narrative provenance tier. Every Layer 2 concept extends a Layer 1 (Workflow Governance) concept through the kernel's named seams. The agent is outside the trust boundary -- the WOS Processor enforces all governance. WOS MUST NOT alter core Formspec processing semantics.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosAIIntegration.description: WOS AI Integration specification version marker. MUST be '1.0'. Identifies this document as a WOS AI Integration Document and pins the schema version. Processors MUST reject documents with an unrecognized value. Corresponds to Layer 2 of the WOS three-layer governance model. See AI Integration §1.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker and version pin for WOS AI Integration documents

targetWorkflow.description: URI of the WOS Kernel Document this AI integration document targets. MUST match the 'url' property of the target Kernel Document. Used by processors to resolve the kernel's named seams (actorExtension, provenanceLayer, lifecycleHook) through which AI governance is applied. An AI Integration Document without a resolvable targetWorkflow MUST be rejected by conformant processors. See AI Integration §2.

x-lm.intent: Binds this AI integration document to a specific kernel workflow

version.description: Version of this AI Integration Document. RECOMMENDED to use semantic versioning (major.minor.patch). Version changes MUST be recorded in provenance when the document is used as part of an active workflow instance.

title.description: Human-readable name for this AI integration configuration. Used in administrative dashboards, audit reports, and governance review tooling to identify this document.

description.description: Human-readable description of how AI agents participate in this workflow. Included in governance documentation and audit packages to provide context for reviewers.
examples: Extends the benefits adjudication workflow with AI-assisted document extraction and eligibility pre-screening.

defaultAutonomy.description: Default autonomy level for agent actions in this workflow (AI Integration S5). Autonomy is a property of the action site, not the agent. Individual actions MAY override this default. The effective autonomy MUST NOT exceed the impact-level cap.

agents.description: Agent declarations registered via the actorExtension seam (AI Integration S3).

x-lm.intent: Registers AI agents as actors in the workflow with identity, type taxonomy, and capabilities

deonticConstraints.description: Deontic constraint framework governing agent behavior (AI Integration S4). Four constraint types from OASIS LegalRuleML: permissions, prohibitions, obligations, rights. Evaluated after contract validation, before output commit. The WOS Processor is the Policy Enforcement Point.

prohibitions.reason: Agent MUST NOT issue final denial on rights-impacting case

x-lm.intent: Structural governance boundary for all agent outputs -- the deontic constraint framework

confidenceFloor.description: Workflow-level minimum confidence threshold (AI Integration S7.4). Part of the enforcement ordering (step 4).

fallbackChain.description: Default fallback chain for agent invocation failures (AI Integration S8). MUST terminate in escalateToHuman or fail. MUST NOT cycle.

x-lm.intent: Guarantees graceful degradation -- every agent failure has a defined resolution path

oversightExtensions.description: AI-specific extensions to Layer 1 review protocols (AI Integration S10). Attach via lifecycleHook on review-tagged transitions.

volumeConstraints.description: Rate limits on autonomous agent actions (AI Integration S11). Part of the enforcement ordering (step 5).

reviewSampling.description: Agent-specific review sampling extending Layer 1 quality controls (AI Integration S11.2). Part of the enforcement ordering (step 6).

agentDisclosure.description: Agent disclosure requirements extending Layer 1 due process (AI Integration S12). Attach via lifecycleHook on adverse-decision-tagged transitions.

narrativeTier.description: Configuration for the Narrative provenance tier (AI Integration S13). Attach via provenanceLayer seam.

driftDetection.description: Decision drift detection configuration (AI Integration S9).

assistGovernanceProxy.description: WOS Assist Governance Proxy configuration (AI Integration S14). A WOS construct consuming the Formspec Assist protocol (Assist S2.1).

id.description: Unique agent identifier within this AI Integration Document. Used to reference this agent from deontic constraints, fallback chains, agent-specific overrides, and WOS Agent Config sidecars. MUST be unique across all agents in the document.

type.description: Actor type discriminator. MUST be 'agent' for AI agent declarations. Registers this actor via the kernel actorExtension seam so that WOS Processor governance rules apply. Human actors declared in the kernel use 'human'; this field distinguishes AI agents from all other actor categories. See AI Integration §3.1.

x-lm.intent: Registers this actor as an agent via the kernel actorExtension seam

agentType.description: Agent type taxonomy (AI Integration S3.2). 'deterministic': rules engines, output is a pure function of input. 'statistical': classical ML, bounded output space, calibratable. 'generative': LLMs, unbounded output, non-deterministic, injection-vulnerable.

x-lm.intent: Governs the applicable governance regime -- generative agents require the strongest controls

modelIdentifier.description: Identifier of the model powering this agent. Recorded in provenance for every invocation. For commercial LLMs use the provider's model name; for custom models use an organization-scoped identifier. This field MUST be updated whenever the underlying model changes.

modelVersion.description: Version string for the model powering this agent. Together with modelIdentifier, uniquely identifies the exact model artifact. Recorded in provenance for every invocation to support reproducibility and audit.

description.description: Human-readable description of this agent's role and behavior in the workflow. Shown in governance review tooling and audit reports.
examples: Extracts income data from uploaded tax documents.
Pre-screens applications for likely eligibility based on verified data.

capabilities.description: Declared capabilities with I/O contracts (AI Integration S3.3).

modelVersionPolicy.description: Model version policy (AI Integration S3.4). 'pinned': exactly the declared version. 'approved': any version from an approved list. 'latest': latest available, version recorded in provenance.

confidenceDecay.description: Agent-specific confidence decay configuration (AI Integration S7.5).

fallbackChain.description: Agent-specific fallback chain. Overrides the workflow-level fallback chain for this agent.

items.description: Agent identifier that this agent is permitted to invoke at autonomous level. MUST match the 'id' of another AgentDeclaration in this document. Creates a declared dependency graph used for cascade governance analysis.

cascadingInvocations.description: Agent IDs that this agent may invoke at autonomous level (AI Integration S3.7). Required when cascading autonomous agents are used.

deonticConstraints.description: Agent-level deontic constraints (AI Integration S4.7). Apply to all invocations of this agent.

id.description: Capability identifier. Referenced from deontic constraints and output contract validation rules. MUST be unique within the parent agent's capabilities array.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this capability does, including what inputs it consumes and what outputs it produces. Displayed in governance review tooling to help auditors understand the agent's functional scope.
examples: Extract structured income data from uploaded tax documents and pay stubs.
Pre-screen application for likely eligibility using verified income and household data.

inputContractRef.description: Formspec Definition or JSON Schema URI for input. Uses headless contract pattern (Core S2.3).

outputContractRef.description: Formspec Definition or JSON Schema URI for output. Agent output is validated against this contract (Formspec-as-validator, S6).

permissions.description: Permissions bounding what agents are allowed to produce (AI Integration S4.2).

prohibitions.description: Prohibitions forbidding specified agent outputs or actions (AI Integration S4.3).

obligations.description: Obligations requiring agents to include specified content (AI Integration S4.4).

rights.description: Rights specifying what agents are entitled to receive as input (AI Integration S4.5).

id.description: Unique permission identifier within this deontic constraints block. Referenced in violation provenance records and audit logs to identify which permission was evaluated.

items.description: Field path (relative to the agent's output object) that the agent is permitted to populate. The WOS Processor rejects output containing fields not in this list when a permission constraint is active.

allowedFields.description: Fields the agent is permitted to produce.

field.description: Specific output field path that this permission constrains. Used together with 'bounds' to restrict the permissible value range for a single field rather than listing an entire output schema.

bounds.description: FEL expression defining value bounds for the field identified by 'field'. Evaluated with 'value' bound to the field's actual output value. Violation fires the onViolation action.

bypassable.description: Whether an authorized actor may bypass this constraint with structured rationale (AI Integration S4.7).

id.description: Unique prohibition identifier within this deontic constraints block. Referenced in violation provenance records and audit logs when the prohibition fires.

condition.description: FEL expression defining the forbidden condition. Evaluated against the agent output and workflow instance context after contract validation. When the expression evaluates to true, the prohibition is triggered and the onViolation action fires. MUST be deterministic given the same inputs. See AI Integration §4.3 for evaluation ordering and null-propagation rules.

x-lm.intent: The guard expression that triggers this prohibition

reason.description: Human-readable explanation. Included in provenance when triggered.
examples: Agent may not render final denial in rights-impacting workflow.

bypassable.description: Whether an authorized actor may bypass this constraint with structured rationale (AI Integration S4.7).

id.description: Unique obligation identifier within this deontic constraints block. Referenced in violation provenance records and audit logs when the obligation check fires.

requirement.description: FEL expression defining the required condition that the agent output MUST satisfy. Evaluated after contract validation. When the expression evaluates to false, the obligation is unmet and the onViolation action fires. Agents that cannot guarantee this condition should be configured at a lower autonomy level. See AI Integration §4.4 and null-propagation rules §4.9.

x-lm.intent: The guard expression that checks this obligation

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why this obligation exists and what regulatory or policy authority requires it. Included in provenance when the obligation is unmet.

bypassable.description: Whether an authorized actor may bypass this constraint with structured rationale (AI Integration S4.7).

id.description: Unique right identifier within this deontic constraints block. Referenced in provenance and audit records to document what data entitlements the agent was granted for each invocation.

entitlement.description: Path or reference specifying the data the agent is entitled to receive.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this right entitles the agent to receive and why it is required for the agent to fulfill its role. Included in governance documentation.
examples: Agent must receive application data as input.

ViolationAction.description: Enforcement action for a deontic constraint violation (AI Integration S4.6). Restriction ordering: reject > escalateToHuman > switchToAssistive > flag.

NullBehavior.description: Override for null propagation behavior (AI Integration S4.9). When omitted, determined by the kernel's impactLevel.

threshold.description: Minimum acceptable confidence for the declared autonomy level.

onViolation.description: Enforcement action taken when the agent's confidence score falls below the configured threshold. 'escalateToHuman': route to a human reviewer via the fallback chain. 'reject': hard-fail the invocation and abort the action.

enabled.description: Whether time-based confidence decay is active for this agent. When true, the effective confidence score degrades over time using the configured halfLife. Used for agents whose outputs may become stale as case data evolves.

halfLife.description: ISO 8601 duration after which the effective confidence score halves relative to the originally reported score. Shorter half-lives force earlier re-invocation for time-sensitive data. Applies only when 'enabled' is true.

event.description: Workflow event path that triggers an immediate confidence reduction. Dot-notation path relative to the workflow instance. When this event fires, the agent's effective confidence is multiplied by the decayFactor immediately, independent of the time-based halfLife decay.

decayFactor.description: Multiplicative decay factor (0-1). Effective confidence is multiplied by this factor when the event fires.

action.description: Fallback action type that determines how to handle an agent invocation failure at this level of the chain (AI Integration S8.3). 'escalateToHuman': route to a human reviewer via taskRef. 'retry': retry the same agent up to maxRetries times. 'alternateAgent': invoke the agent identified by alternateAgentRef. 'fail': abort with an error. A valid chain MUST terminate in 'escalateToHuman' or 'fail'.

x-lm.intent: Determines what happens when an agent invocation fails

taskRef.description: Task identifier to create in the kernel when action is 'escalateToHuman'. MUST reference a task declared in the target Kernel Document's task registry so it can be assigned to a human reviewer in the workflow.

maxRetries.description: Maximum number of retry attempts before this fallback level is exhausted and the next level in the chain is tried. Applies only when action is 'retry'.

backoff.description: Retry interval growth strategy. 'fixed': constant interval. 'exponential': doubles each attempt (recommended for transient failures). 'linear': increases by initialInterval each attempt.

initialInterval.description: ISO 8601 duration for the first retry interval. Subsequent intervals are computed from this base according to the backoff strategy.

alternateAgentRef.description: Agent identifier to invoke as the alternate when action is 'alternateAgent'. MUST match the 'id' of another AgentDeclaration in this document. Governance constraints for the alternate agent are applied independently.

assignTo.description: Actor identifier to assign the escalated task to when action is 'escalateToHuman'. MUST match an actor declared in the kernel's actorRegistry. When omitted, assignment follows the kernel's default task routing rules.

suppressAgentOutput.description: When true and the independentFirst review protocol is active, hide agent output until the reviewer's independent assessment is recorded (AI Integration S10.2). Prevents anchoring bias where reviewers defer to the agent rather than forming their own judgment.

presentation.description: Presentation properties for AI-assisted review steps.

showConfidence.description: Display the agent's overall confidence score to the human reviewer. Enables reviewers to calibrate their scrutiny level — e.g., applying more care when confidence is near the floor. Ignored when suppressAgentOutput is true until the independent assessment is submitted.

showAlternatives.description: Display the agent's alternative recommendations alongside its primary recommendation so reviewers can evaluate the full decision space rather than just approving or rejecting the top result.

highlightLowConfidenceFields.description: Visually highlight individual output fields whose per-field confidence score falls below the configured threshold so reviewers focus attention on the weakest parts of the agent output first.

showDiffFromIndependent.description: Highlight differences between the reviewer's independent assessment and the agent's recommendation after the independent assessment is submitted. Only meaningful with independentFirst protocol; ignored otherwise.

maxAutonomousPerHour.description: Maximum number of fully autonomous agent actions permitted in any rolling 60-minute window across this workflow. When the limit is reached additional autonomous actions are queued or escalated to supervisory per the fallback chain.

maxAutonomousPerDay.description: Maximum number of fully autonomous agent actions permitted in any rolling 24-hour window across this workflow. Provides a daily capacity ceiling complementing the per-hour rate limit.

rate.description: Fraction of agent actions to sample for human review. 1.0 means every action is reviewed; 0.0 disables sampling. MUST be increased when drift monitoring detects accuracy degradation.

method.description: Sampling method (AI Integration S11.2). 'stratified': by agent type, confidence, or case characteristics. 'adversarial': targeted sampling of cases likely to reveal agent weaknesses.

scope.description: Whether sampling rate applies across all agents in the workflow ('workflow') or independently per agent ('agent'). Use 'agent' scope to apply different rates to each agent's invocations.

discloseThatAgentAssisted.description: Disclose that an AI system assisted in the determination when an adverse decision notice is issued (AI Integration S12.2). MUST be true for rights-impacting workflows per OMB M-24-10 and EU AI Act Art. 13. When true, the WOS Processor injects an AI-assistance disclosure statement into adverse-decision-tagged transition output.

x-lm.intent: Controls whether AI involvement is disclosed in adverse decision notices

discloseModelIdentity.description: Whether to include the model identifier and version in the adverse decision disclosure notice. Enabling this increases transparency but may expose vendor or model information the agency wishes to protect.

discloseConfidence.description: Whether to include the agent's overall confidence score in the adverse decision disclosure notice. Enables affected parties to understand how certain the AI system was when it contributed to the determination.

enabled.description: Whether agents are required to produce Narrative provenance tier records for their outputs. When true, agent outputs lacking a Narrative record are treated as incomplete by the WOS Processor. See AI Integration §13.

items.description: Transition tag that requires a Narrative tier record from any agent actor involved in the transition. MUST match a tag defined in the target Kernel Document's transition definitions.

requiredForTags.description: Transition tags that require Narrative tier records from agent actors.

trainingDataDisclosure.description: Whether agents must disclose training data characteristics in their provenance records (AI Integration S9.2). When true, agents must include training data demographic composition, date range, and source type in their Narrative tier records.

optimizationObjectiveDisclosure.description: Whether agents must disclose their optimization objective (e.g., precision, recall, F1) in provenance records. Enables downstream auditors to evaluate whether the optimization target is aligned with the workflow's policy goals.

enabled.description: Whether rubber-stamp detection monitoring is active for human reviewers of agent outputs. When true, the WOS Processor tracks reviewer engagement metrics and flags patterns indicating reviewers are approving agent outputs without meaningful scrutiny.

minReviewTime.description: ISO 8601 duration below which a review is flagged as potentially rubber-stamped.

maxAgreementRate.description: Agreement rate above which reviewer engagement is flagged as declining.

evaluationWindow.description: ISO 8601 duration of the rolling window over which rubber-stamp metrics are aggregated. Reviews older than this window are excluded from the engagement rate calculation.

enabled.description: Whether the WOS Assist Governance Proxy is active for this workflow. When true, the proxy intercepts Formspec Assist tool invocations and applies WOS deontic constraints before the tool call proceeds. See AI Integration §14.

toolCategories.description: Per-tool-category governance configuration. The proxy consumes the Assist protocol (Assist S2.1, S7) and applies WOS deontic constraints.

category.description: Tool category identifier as defined in the Formspec Assist protocol tool taxonomy. Groups tools by access pattern so deontic constraints can apply at the category level rather than requiring one rule per tool.

constraints.description: Deontic constraints specific to this tool category.

maxInvocationsPerSession.description: Maximum number of tool invocations in this category permitted per workflow session instance. Prevents runaway tool usage by bounded-autonomy agents. When the limit is reached, further invocations in the category are blocked and the configured deontic violation action fires.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosKernel': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
